reason: DB access unavailable in this run. The Linux Cowork sandbox returned 'No space left on device' on every bash invocation, so politics.db could not be opened to read the politicians table or the politician_events join. Per the prior-run pattern documented in cowork_outbox/_log.md (entry for 2026-05-03 step2), the consistency scoring cannot proceed without the joined event data and so the 138 rows are NOT being scored from secondary inference; doing so would invent a verdict.
next_actions_for_windows_side: Re-run python main.py score-consistency --date 2026-05-04 from the Windows side, where politics.db is openable directly without WAL contention.
Verify the consistency_scores table is not still in the disk-malformed state flagged on 2026-05-01; if it is, drop and recreate before re-running.
If the table is healthy, the scoring run should complete in under five minutes for the 138-id list.
